description.para: Each of the options is bit field, so more than one options can be specified.

briefdescription.para: External clock source.
Clock on trailing edge for external clock.
Interval mode.
Decrement the counter.
Match mode.
No waveform output.
Waveform polarity.
Mask to detect main TTC node.
This function starts the counter/timer without resetting the counter value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the counter/timer.

detaileddescription.para: This macro may be called at any time to stop the counter. The counter holds the last value until it is reset, restarted or enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether the timer counter has already started.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance

simplesect.para: Non-zero if the device has started, '0' otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the current 16-bit counter value.

detaileddescription.para: It may be called at any time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

simplesect.para: zynq:16 bit counter value. zynq ultrascale+mpsoc:32 bit counter value.
C-style signature: zynq: u16 XTtcPs_GetCounterValue(XTtcPs *InstancePtr) zynq ultrascale+mpsoc: u32 XTtcPs_GetCounterValue(XTtcPs *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: This function sets the interval value to be used in interval mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
is the 16-bit value to be set in the interval register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XTtcPs_SetInterval(XTtcPs *InstancePtr, XInterval Value)

briefdescription.para: This function gets the interval value from the interval register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

simplesect.para: zynq:16 bit interval value. zynq ultrascale+mpsoc:32 bit interval value.
C-style signature: zynq: u16 XTtcPs_GetInterval(XTtcPs *InstancePtr) zynq ultrascale+mpsoc: u32 XTtcPs_GetInterval(XTtcPs *InstancePtr)

briefdescription.para: This macro resets the count register.

detaileddescription.para: It may be called at any time. The counter is reset to either 0 or 0xFFFF, or the interval value, depending on the increment/decrement mode. The state of the counter, as started or stopped, is not affected by calling reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
defines which interrupt should be enabled. Constants are defined in xttcps_hw.h as XTTCPS_IXR_*. This is a bit mask, all set bits will be enabled, cleared bits will not be disabled.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XTtcPs_EnableInterrupts(XTtcPs *InstancePtr, u32 InterruptMask)

briefdescription.para: This function disables the interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
defines which interrupt should be disabled. Constants are defined in xttcps_hw.h as XTTCPS_IXR_*. This is a bit mask, all set bits will be disabled, cleared bits will not be disabled.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XTtcPs_DisableInterrupts(XTtcPs *InstancePtr, u32 InterruptMask)

briefdescription.para: This function reads the interrupt status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the interrupt status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
defines which interrupt should be cleared. Constants are defined in xttcps_hw.h as XTTCPS_IXR_*. This is a bit mask, all set bits will be cleared, cleared bits will not be cleared.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XTtcPs_ClearInterruptStatus(XTtcPs *InstancePtr, u32 InterruptMask)

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XTtcPs_StatusHandler)(const void *CallBackRef, u32 StatusEvent)
memberdef.name: XTtcPs_StatusHandler

briefdescription.para: This typedef contains interval count and Match register value.
Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xttcps.h for the definition of XTtcPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XTtcPs instance such that the driver is ready to use.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes a single timer counter in the triple timer counter function block.

The state of the device after initialization is:
Overflow Mode
Internal (pclk) selected
Counter disabled
All Interrupts disabled
Output waveforms disabled

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific TTC device.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, then use ConfigPtr->BaseAddress for this parameter, passing the physical address instead.

para.verbatim: - XST_SUCCESS if the initialization is successful. - XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device is started. It must be stopped to re-initialize.

simplesect.para: Device has to be stopped first to call this function to initialize it.

briefdescription.para: This routine releases resources of XTtcPs instance/driver.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if node release was successful
XST_FAILURE if node release was fail, Node won't be released if any other counter/counters in that TTC in use.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the match registers.

detaileddescription.para: The match 0 register is special. If the waveform output mode is enabled, the waveform will change polarity when the count matches the value in the match 0 register. The polarity of the waveform output can also be set using the XTtcPs_SetOptions() function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
is the index to the match register to be set. Valid values are: 0 - 2.
is the 16-bit value to be set in the match register. Valid Values are: (For Zynq): 0 - ((2^16)-1) (For Zynq UltraScale + MpSoc) and Versal: 0 - ((2^32) - 1)

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the value of the match registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
is the index to the match register to be set. There are three match registers are there. Valid values are: 0 - 2.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the prescaler enable bit and if needed sets the prescaler bits in the control register.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
PrescalerValue
is a number from 0-16 that sets the prescaler to use. If the parameter is 0 - 15, use a prescaler on the clock of 2^(PrescalerValue+1), or 2-65536. If the parameter is XTTCPS_CLK_CNTRL_PS_DISABLE, do not use a prescaler.
Valid values are: 0 - 15

briefdescription.para: This function gets the input clock prescaler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

para.preformatted: The value(n) from which the prescalar value is calculated as 2^(n+1). Some example values are given below : Value Prescaler 0 2 1 4 N 2^(n+1) 15 65536 16 1

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the interval value as well as the prescaler value for a given frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
is the requested output frequency for the device. valid values are: 1 - (2^32)-1
is the interval value for the given frequency, it is the output value for this function.
is the prescaler value for the given frequency, it is the output value for this function.

simplesect.para: Upon successful calculation for the given frequency, Interval and Prescaler carry the settings for the timer counter; Upon unsuccessful calculation, Interval and Prescaler are set to 0xFF(FF) for their maximum values to signal the caller of failure. Therefore, caller needs to check the return interval or prescaler values for whether the function has succeeded.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the options for the TTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

contains the specified options to be set. This is a bit mask where a 1 means to turn the option on, and a 0 means to turn the option off. One or more bit values may be contained in the mask. See the bit definitions named XTTCPS_*_OPTION in the file xttcps.h.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if options are successfully set.
XST_FAILURE if any of the options are unknown.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the settings for the options for the TTC device.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
The return u32 contains the specified options that are set. This is a bit mask where a '1' means the option is on, and a'0' means the option is off. One or more bit values may be contained in the mask. See the bit definitions named XTTCPS_*_OPTION in the file xttcps.h.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

para.verbatim: - XST_SUCCESS if successful - XST_FAILURE indicates a register did not read or write correctly

simplesect.para: This test fails if it is not called right after initialization.

briefdescription.para: Handles interrupts by resetting the counter value and clearing the status register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.

briefdescription.para: Sets the status callback function, the status handler, which the driver calls when it encounters conditions that should be reported to upper layer software.

detaileddescription.para: The handler executes in an interrupt context, so it must minimize the amount of processing performed. One of the following status events is passed to the status handler.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XTtcPs instance.
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
FuncPointer
is the pointer to the callback function.
None.
The handler is called within interrupt context, so it should do its work quickly and queue potentially time-consuming work to a task-level thread.
